post.summary: Receive a webhook push event and trigger a project pull.
post.description:
This endpoint is **unauthenticated** -- it relies on HMAC signature
verification against the per-project webhook secret.

Supported providers: `github`, `gitea`, `forgejo`, `gitlab`.

# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::NotFound`] when no webhook secret is configured,
[`ApiError::Forbidden`] when signature verification fails,
[`ApiError::BadRequest`] for unsupported providers or missing git URL,
or [`ApiError::Internal`] on git operation failures.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: Git host provider (github, gitea, forgejo, gitlab)

requestBody.description: Raw webhook payload from the git host

400.description: Bad request (unsupported provider or project has no git URL)

404.description: Project not found or no webhook secret configured

ArchKind.description: CPU architecture a service needs. Mirrors the arch half of an OCI platform.

AuditEntry.description:
A recorded audit log entry capturing who did what, when, and to which
resource.

id.description: UUID identifier of this audit entry.

resource_id.description: The specific resource id, if applicable.

resource_kind.description:
The kind of resource acted upon (e.g. `"deployment"`, `"user"`,
`"project"`).

user_id.description: The user who performed the action.

BackendGroupInfo.description: A load-balancer backend group for one service.

BackendInfo.description: Information about a single backend in a load-balancer group.

healthy.description: Whether the backend is currently healthy.

BridgeInfo.description: Where on a specific node a service's overlay terminates.

gateway.description:
Gateway IP within the subnet (first usable address); the bridge's
own L3 address. Containers' default route points here.

name.description: Linux bridge name (`br-svc-<hash>`), max 15 chars per IFNAMSIZ.

node_id.description:
Node identifier (stringified to avoid leaking `NodeId` type details
across crate boundaries).

subnet.description: CIDR of the service subnet assigned to this node.

BridgeNetwork.description: A user-defined bridge or overlay network that containers can attach to.

driver.description: Driver backing the network (bridge vs. overlay).

internal.description:
If true, containers attached to this network cannot reach the outside
world — only other containers on the same network.

labels.description: Arbitrary key/value labels for filtering and grouping.

aliases.description: DNS aliases the container can be reached by on this network.

ipv4.description: Assigned IPv4 address on the network (if any).

allOf.description:
Core network metadata. Flattened so the JSON stays close to the
list-item shape returned by `list_container_networks`.

attached_containers.description: Containers currently attached to the network.

runtime.description: Use runtime template instead of Dockerfile

tags.description: Tags to apply to the image

BuildRequestWithContext.description: Build request with server-side context path

context_path.description: Path to the build context on the server

runtime.description: Use runtime template instead of Dockerfile

errors.description: Per-line errors. Empty when every line parsed and stored cleanly.

CertInfo.description: Information about a loaded TLS certificate.

fingerprint.description: SHA-256 fingerprint, if metadata is available.

needs_renewal.description: Whether the certificate needs renewal (within 30 days of expiry).

not_after.description: Certificate expiry (ISO-8601), if metadata is available.

not_before.description: Certificate validity start (ISO-8601), if metadata is available.

oneOf.description: CPU architecture of the joining agent. Same legacy semantics as `os`.

cpu_total.description: Total CPU cores on the joining node

labels.description:
Free-form labels advertised by the joining agent, used for
`NodeSelector` placement matching. Empty on legacy clients.

oneOf.description:
Operating system of the joining agent. `None` = legacy client that did
not report platform info.

secrets_pubkey.description:
Joiner's 32-byte X25519 pubkey for sealed-box DEK wrapping.
Present on Phase-1+ joiners; absent on legacy clients (in which
case the leader treats the node as not eligible to host
replicated-secret ciphertext until it re-joins with a pubkey).

dek_generation.description:
Cluster DEK generation that `wrapped_dek` was sealed under. Lets
the joiner detect rotation drift if it re-joins after a revocation
rotated the cluster DEK. `None` when `wrapped_dek` is `None`.

join_secret.description:
Cluster-wide HMAC join secret. Returned to authenticated joiners
so they can derive the same internal RPC bearer as the leader.
`None` on legacy responses from older leaders.

node_jwt.description:
Node JWT minted by the leader for this joiner — `roles: ["node"]`,
`node_id` set. Used to authenticate inter-node calls separately
from any user identity. `None` on legacy responses.

overlay_ip.description: Assigned overlay IP for the new node

role.description: Role assigned to this node: "voter" or "learner"

slice_cidr.description:
Per-node slice CIDR assigned by the leader (e.g. "10.200.42.0/28").
Empty string if the leader is not slice-aware yet.

warnings.description:
Server-side advisory warnings to surface to the operator/CLI.

Examples: "your token format is deprecated and will be removed in
release X.Y", "consider rotating the signing key, last rotated N
days ago". Present-but-empty means "no warnings"; serialized as
`null` (skip-if-none) when there are none.

wrapped_dek.description:
Sealed-box-wrapped copy of the cluster DEK addressed to the
joiner's `secrets_pubkey`. The joiner unwraps with its node X25519
private key and holds the DEK in zeroized memory. `None` on legacy
responses or when the joiner did not provide a `secrets_pubkey`.

ClusterNodeSummary.description: Summary of a cluster node for listing.

api_endpoint.description:
API endpoint as `advertise_addr:api_port` (e.g., "127.0.0.1:19110").
Distinct from `address` which holds the Raft RPC endpoint.

cpu_total.description: Total CPU cores on this node

is_leader.description: Whether this node is the Raft leader

overlay_ip.description: Overlay network IP assigned to this node

registered_at.description: When the node was registered (Unix timestamp ms)

role.description: Role in the Raft cluster: "leader", "voter", or "learner"

ClusterPeer.description: Summary of an existing cluster peer returned in join response.

ClusterUpgradeRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/v1/cluster/upgrade`.

Drives a leader-coordinated rolling daemon-binary upgrade across the
cluster. The leader walks every follower in ascending `node_id` order
(and finally upgrades itself), instructing each to download the target
`zlayer` release and restart. Followers come back on the same advertise
address and re-join Raft; the leader waits for each one to be healthy
again before moving on.

cooldown_secs.description: Seconds to pause between node upgrades after each follower comes back healthy.

strict.description: Abort the rollout if any follower fails to come back healthy.

version.description: Target zlayer version (e.g. "v0.12.0"). Defaults to latest GitHub release if absent.

ClusterUpgradeResult.description: Final result of a rolling upgrade attempt.

errors.description: Nodes that failed mid-upgrade (one entry per failure).

skipped.description: Nodes intentionally skipped (e.g. unreachable, missing api endpoint).

upgraded.description: Nodes that successfully restarted on the new binary.

ClusterUpgradeSelfRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/v1/cluster/upgrade-self`.

Triggers a daemon-binary self-upgrade on this node. Used by the CLI
after `cluster_upgrade` finishes the follower walk, so the leader can
upgrade itself last. The handler re-enters
`internal_upgrade_start` over localhost using the configured internal
token, so all the existing self-update / sentinel / exit-75 plumbing
is reused.

version.description: Target version (e.g. `"v0.12.0"`). Defaults to latest GitHub release.

aliases.description: Optional DNS aliases on this network.

ipv4_address.description:
Optional static IPv4 to pin this container to. Validated as
[`std::net::Ipv4Addr`].

ContainerChangeEntry.description:
One row of `GET /api/v1/containers/{id}/changes` (Docker compat shape).

Mirrors Docker's `{"Path": "/foo", "Kind": 0}` body:
`Kind` is a numeric enum where `0 = Modified`, `1 = Added`, `2 = Deleted`.

Path.description: Path inside the container that changed (absolute, e.g. `/etc/hosts`).

ContainerEvent.description: A container lifecycle event published on the bus.

at.description:
Wall-clock time the event was emitted.

Serialized as an RFC 3339 string on the wire.

exit_code.description:
Exit code, when known. Populated for `Die` events where a wait has
already resolved; otherwise `None`.

id.description: Container identifier (the API's id string, not the raw runtime id).

kind.description: What kind of transition this event represents.

labels.description:
Labels on the container at the time of the event. Used by subscribers
to filter via the `label=k=v` query param (AND semantics).

reason.description:
Free-form human-readable reason. For `Die`, may indicate "stopped",
"killed", "oom-killed"; for `Oom`, the OOM detail; for `Health`, may
echo the probe failure message.

status.description:
Health status, only populated for `Health` events (e.g. "healthy",
"unhealthy", "starting").

ContainerExecRequest.description: Exec request for running a command in a container

ContainerHealthInfo.description:
Runtime-native health snapshot on [`ContainerInfo::health`].

Sourced from bollard's `ContainerState.health` for Docker-backed
containers. The internal `HealthMonitor` in
`crates/zlayer-agent/src/health.rs` drives service-level health events
against user-configured health specs; for standalone containers the API
reports the runtime-native status instead so images with a baked-in
`HEALTHCHECK` still surface correctly.

failing_streak.description: Consecutive failing probe count, when the runtime tracks it.

last_output.description: Output from the most recent failing probe, when available.

status.description:
One of `"none"`, `"starting"`, `"healthy"`, `"unhealthy"` (Docker
`HealthStatusEnum`). Empty / missing upstream values normalise to
`"none"`.

ContainerInfo.description: Container information returned by the API

exit_code.description:
Most-recent exit code. `None` for containers still running and for
containers that have never exited.

oneOf.description:
Runtime-native health status, when the container image declares a
`HEALTHCHECK` (or equivalent). `None` when the runtime doesn't track
health for this container.

ipv4.description:
Primary IPv4 address (first non-empty IP across attached networks).
Docker's `bridge` network is preferred when present.

networks.description:
Networks this container is attached to, with per-network aliases
and IPv4. Empty when the runtime doesn't surface network detail.

ports.description:
Published port mappings (container → host). Populated from the
runtime's inspect response; empty when the runtime doesn't expose
port-level detail or the container has no published ports.

ContainerPortBinding.description: One host binding inside a [`ContainerPortResponse`] entry.

HostIp.description:
Host IP that maps to the container port. Empty / `"0.0.0.0"` means
"any IPv4 address".

HostPort.description: Host port (always serialised as a string in Docker's wire format).

ContainerPortResponse.description:
Response body for `GET /api/v1/containers/{id}/port` (Docker compat shape).

Mirrors Docker's `{"Ports": {"80/tcp": [{"HostIp":"...","HostPort":"..."}]}}`
body. Each key is `<container_port>/<protocol>` and the value is the list
of host bindings for that port (or `null` when the port is exposed but not
published).

ContainerPruneResponse.description:
Response body for `POST /api/v1/containers/prune` (Docker compat shape).

Docker uses `ContainersDeleted` / `SpaceReclaimed` `PascalCase` fields, so
SDK consumers (and the docker shim) can read the body verbatim.

SpaceReclaimed.description: Bytes reclaimed from the runtime's container storage.

ContainerRestartKind.description: Which flavor of container restart policy to apply.

ContainerRestartPolicy.description:
Container-runtime-level restart policy.

Maps onto Docker's `HostConfig.RestartPolicy`. Distinct from
[`PanicPolicy`], which governs what `ZLayer` does in response to an
application panic (it does not set a Docker restart policy).

delay.description:
Humantime-formatted delay between restarts (e.g. `"500ms"`,
`"2s"`). Accepted for forward-compatibility but currently ignored
by the Docker backend: bollard's `RestartPolicy` has no per-kind
delay field. When set, the runtime emits a warning.

max_attempts.description:
For `on_failure` only: maximum number of restart attempts before
giving up. Ignored by other kinds. `None` means "retry forever".

memory_limit.description: Memory limit in bytes (`u64::MAX` if unlimited)

memory_percent.description: Memory usage as percentage of limit

ContainerTopResponse.description:
Response body for `GET /api/v1/containers/{id}/top` (Docker compat shape).

Wire field names use Docker's `Titles` / `Processes` casing so the
shim can pass the body through untouched.

Processes.description:
One row per process inside the container. Each row has the same
length as `titles`.

ContainerUpdateRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/v1/containers/{id}/update`.

Mirrors Docker Engine's `POST /containers/{id}/update` body 1:1 so the
`zlayer-docker` compatibility shim can pass the wire payload straight
through. Every field is optional — only the fields present on the wire
are applied; unset fields are left untouched on the running container.

Field naming uses Docker's `PascalCase` on the wire (`CpuShares`,
`Memory`, ...) and `snake_case` on the Rust side. Subset of the full
Docker schema: `ZLayer` supports the resource knobs (cpu, memory, pids,
blkio) plus `RestartPolicy`. Windows-only fields (`CpuCount`,
`IOMaximumIOps`) and ulimits/devices are accepted on the wire but
silently ignored by the Linux runtimes.

CpuQuota.description:
CPU CFS quota in microseconds. Together with `cpu_period` defines
the fraction of a CPU the container may use.

CpuShares.description:
Relative CPU weight (cgroup `cpu.weight` or `cpu.shares`). Range
2-262144 on cgroup v2; 2-262144 mapped from 1-10000 on v1.

KernelMemory.description:
Kernel memory limit in bytes (deprecated upstream; accepted for
wire compatibility).

Memory.description: Memory limit in bytes. Set `0` to remove the limit.

MemoryReservation.description:
Soft memory limit in bytes. The kernel reclaims pages above this
reservation when the host comes under memory pressure.

MemorySwap.description:
Total memory limit (memory + swap) in bytes. `-1` removes the swap
limit, matching Docker semantics.

PidsLimit.description: PIDs limit. Set `0` or `-1` for unlimited.

oneOf.description:
New restart policy. When present, replaces the container's stored
restart policy. Docker applies this asynchronously: the next time
the supervisor decides whether to restart, it consults the new
policy.

ContainerUpdateResponse.description:
Response body for `POST /api/v1/containers/{id}/update`.

Mirrors Docker's `{"Warnings": [...]}` shape so the compat layer
passes the body through verbatim. `Warnings` is always present (even
if empty) for wire compatibility with clients that match the field
presence, not just its contents.

Warnings.description:
Human-readable warnings emitted by the runtime while applying the
update — e.g. `"kernel memory limit is deprecated"`.

ContainerUpdateRestartPolicy.description:
Restart policy entry for [`ContainerUpdateRequest`].

Mirrors Docker's `HostConfig.RestartPolicy` shape so the Docker compat
layer can pass the wire payload through unchanged. `name` accepts the
same set of strings as `docker run --restart`: `""`, `"no"`, `"always"`,
`"unless-stopped"`, or `"on-failure"`. `maximum_retry_count` is only
honoured when `name == "on-failure"`.

MaximumRetryCount.description:
Maximum number of retries before giving up (only used with
`on-failure`). When `0` or omitted, retries are unbounded.

Message.description: Human-readable description of why the wait failed.

ContainerWaitDockerResponse.description:
Docker-shaped wait response returned by
`POST /api/v1/containers/{id}/wait`.

Mirrors Docker Engine's `/containers/{id}/wait` body 1:1: a
`StatusCode` field plus an optional `Error` envelope. Used by the
`zlayer-docker` compatibility shim and any SDK callers that consume
the Docker shape directly. The richer
[`ContainerWaitResponse`] (returned by the legacy `GET` endpoint) is
preserved for clients that need the `reason` / `signal` / `finished_at`
classification fields.

oneOf.description:
Optional error envelope surfaced when the wait itself failed
(e.g. the container was removed before reaching `not-running`
when `condition=not-running` was requested). Absent on a normal
exit.

StatusCode.description:
Container exit code (0 = success). When killed by signal `N`,
this is typically `128 + N`, matching Docker's convention.

ContainerWaitResponse.description:
Wait response with container exit code plus optional classification
fields (added in §3.12 of the SDK-fixes spec).

The three optional fields (`reason`, `signal`, `finished_at`) are
additive — clients that only read `exit_code` keep working unchanged.

exit_code.description:
Exit code (0 = success). When the container was killed by signal
`N`, this is typically `128 + N`.

finished_at.description:
RFC3339 timestamp of when the container exited, if reported by
the runtime.

reason.description:
Classification of the exit. One of `"exited"`, `"signal"`,
`"oom_killed"`, or `"runtime_error"`. Absent when the runtime
didn't classify the exit.

signal.description:
Signal name when `reason == "signal"`, e.g. `"SIGKILL"`. Absent
when the runtime couldn't determine it (or the exit wasn't a
signal death).

CreateAccessSessionRequest.description:
Request to create a temporary access session for an existing tunneled service.

Used by `zlayer tunnel access <endpoint>` to ask the daemon to bind a local
TCP listener that proxies to a remote service. The daemon returns the local
address so the CLI can either connect directly (when the daemon and CLI
share a host) or open a second hop forwarder.

endpoint.description:
Service endpoint to access (e.g. "postgres.service.zlayer" or
"host:port"). The daemon resolves this through the overlay network
when configured.

local_port.description:
Optional local port for the daemon to bind. `None` (or `0`) lets the
daemon auto-assign an ephemeral port.

ttl_secs.description:
Time-to-live in seconds for the session. After this expires the
listener is torn down.

CreateAccessSessionResponse.description: Response after creating a temporary access session.

expires_at.description: Unix timestamp at which the session expires.

local_addr.description: Local address the daemon bound for this session, e.g. `127.0.0.1:30042`.

oneOf.description: Driver, defaults to `bridge`.

CreateContainerRequest.description: Request to create and start a container

cap_add.description:
Linux capabilities to add (Docker `--cap-add`). Maps to
`ServiceSpec::capabilities`.

cpu_shares.description: Relative CPU shares (Docker `--cpu-shares`). Default weight is 1024.

cpuset.description:
CPUs that the container is allowed to execute on
(Docker `--cpuset-cpus`).

devices.description: Host devices to expose to the container (Docker `--device`).

dns.description:
Additional DNS servers (maps to Docker's `--dns`). Each entry must be
a plausible IPv4 or IPv6 address.

extra_groups.description:
Additional groups to add to the container process
(Docker `--group-add`).

extra_hosts.description:
Extra `hostname:ip` entries appended to `/etc/hosts` (maps to Docker's
`--add-host`). The special literal `host-gateway` is accepted as the
`ip` half.

oneOf.description:
Optional health check. When omitted, the daemon installs a no-op
placeholder (`HealthCheck::Tcp { port: 0 }`) matching the current
default; the health monitor treats `port == 0` as "skip".

init_container.description:
Run a Docker-supplied init process (PID 1) inside the container
(Docker `--init`). Distinct from `ZLayer`'s pre-start init actions.

ipc_mode.description:
IPC namespace mode (Docker `--ipc`). Accepts e.g. `"host"`,
`"shareable"`, `"private"`, or `"container:<id>"`.

lifecycle.description:
Container lifecycle policy. Carries the `delete_on_exit` knob (Docker
`--rm` / `HostConfig.AutoRemove`) so the daemon can remove terminated
container records and bundles once they exit. Defaults to
[`crate::spec::LifecycleSpec::default()`] (i.e. retain on exit), which
matches the historical behavior for callers that omit the field.

oneOf.description:
Network mode (Docker `--network`). Accepts `"default"`, `"host"`,
`"none"`, `"bridge"`, `"bridge:<name>"`, or `"container:<id>"`. When
omitted, defaults to [`crate::spec::NetworkMode::Default`].

networks.description:
User-defined bridge/overlay networks to attach the newly-created
container to. Each entry references a network by id or name and is
attached after the container is successfully started. If any
attachment fails, the partially-started container is rolled back
(stopped + removed) and the request is failed.

oneOf.description:
Node selection constraints (required / preferred labels). When set on a
daemon that has a cluster handle, the leader places the container on a
node whose labels satisfy the required set; otherwise the field is
ignored and the container is created locally.

oom_kill_disable.description: Disable the OOM killer for the container (Docker `--oom-kill-disable`).

pid_mode.description:
PID namespace mode (Docker `--pid`). Accepts e.g. `"host"` or
`"container:<id>"`.

pids_limit.description:
Maximum number of processes the container may spawn
(Docker `--pids-limit`).

oneOf.description:
Target platform (OS + arch) the container must run on, e.g.
`darwin/arm64`. When set on a clustered daemon, the leader places the
container on a node whose reported platform matches; when no node
matches, the request is rejected. Ignored on single-node daemons.

ports.description:
Published ports (Docker's `-p host:container/proto`). When omitted,
the container is created without any host port publishing.

privileged.description:
Run the container in privileged mode (Docker `--privileged`). When
omitted, defaults to `false`.

read_only_root_fs.description: Mount the container's root filesystem read-only (Docker `--read-only`).

oneOf.description:
Inline Docker/OCI registry credentials used for this pull only. Not
persisted, never logged, never echoed back on a response. When both
`registry_credential_id` and `registry_auth` are set, this field
takes precedence.

registry_credential_id.description:
Id of a persisted registry credential (from
`POST /api/v1/credentials/registry`) to use when pulling the image.
Ignored when [`Self::registry_auth`] is also supplied (inline auth
wins). Requires the daemon to be configured with a credential store
— otherwise the request is rejected with `400`.

oneOf.description: Resource limits (CPU, memory)

Container restart policy (Docker-style). When omitted, the runtime
applies no explicit restart policy (Docker default: `"no"`).

stop_grace_period.description:
Grace period to wait between the stop signal and a forced kill
(Docker `--stop-timeout`). Wire format is a humantime string
(e.g. `"30s"`, `"500ms"`, `"1m"`).

stop_signal.description:
Signal sent to the container's main process to request a graceful
shutdown (Docker `--stop-signal`). Accepts e.g. `"SIGTERM"` or `"15"`.

user.description:
User and group override for the container's main process
(Docker `--user uid:gid`).

description.description: Free-form description shown in the UI.

name.description: Display name. Must be unique within the chosen `project_id` namespace.

value.description: PAT or SSH key content (stored encrypted, never returned).

CreateNodeTunnelRequest.description: Request to create a node-to-node tunnel

local_port.description: Local port on the source node

remote_port.description: Remote port on the destination node

CreateNodeTunnelResponse.description: Response after creating a node-to-node tunnel

auto_deploy.description: Enable automatic deploy on new commits.

oneOf.description: How the project is built.

build_path.description: Relative build path within the repo.

deploy_spec_path.description:
Relative path (inside the cloned repo) to a `DeploymentSpec` YAML
that the workflow `DeployProject` action should apply.

poll_interval_secs.description: Polling interval in seconds (None = no polling).

password.description: Password or token value (stored encrypted, never returned).

CreateSecretRequest.description:
Request to create or update a secret.

`scope` is optional and only honored on the legacy code path — when set
alongside `?environment=`, the request is rejected.

oneOf.description:
Optional per-secret node affinity. `None` (default) = any node may
host the decryptable form. When set, only matching nodes receive a
wrap of the DEK material for this row, and the API gate filters
reads accordingly. Ignored on standalone (non-clustered) daemons.

scope.description:
Optional explicit scope (legacy form). Mutually exclusive with the
`?environment=` query parameter.

value.description: The secret value (will be encrypted at rest).

auto_apply.description: Whether the sync should automatically apply on pull.

delete_missing.description:
Whether `apply` should delete resources on the API that are missing
from the manifest directory. Defaults to `false` (the safer choice).

git_path.description: Path within the project's checkout to scan for resource YAMLs.

CreateTunnelRequest.description: Request to create a new tunnel token

services.description: Optional list of services this tunnel is allowed to expose

CreateTunnelResponse.description: Response after creating a tunnel token

created_at.description: When the tunnel was created (Unix timestamp)

token.description: The tunnel token to use for authentication

name.description:
Variable name (e.g. `"APP_VERSION"`). Must be unique within the chosen
scope.

labels.description: Optional labels to attach to the volume.

size.description:
Optional size hint (humansize format: `"512Mi"`, `"10Gi"`).
Recorded in the sidecar for display and future quota enforcement.

last_run.description: When the job last ran (ISO 8601 format)

DaemonCapabilitiesResponse.description:
Response body for `GET /api/v1/daemon/capabilities`.

Snapshot of the daemon's runtime environment: privilege level, cgroup
scoping, network-related kernel capabilities. The fields mirror
[`DaemonCapabilities`] field-for-field; values are memoised at process
start and do not change at runtime.

can_write_cgroup_root.description:
`true` if the cgroup root's `cgroup.subtree_control` has the owner-
write bit set.

cgroup_parent.description: The cgroup v2 path of the current process, if any.

has_cap_net_admin.description: `true` if `CAP_NET_ADMIN` is present in the process's bounding set.

is_nested.description:
`true` if the process appears to be inside a container (non-root
cgroup v2 path).

is_root.description: `true` if the process is running as uid 0.

mode.description: Coarse classification derived from the boolean fields below.

tun_device_available.description:
`true` if `/dev/net/tun` can be opened r/w without
EACCES/EPERM/ENOENT/ENXIO.

DaemonEvent.description:
A daemon lifecycle event. One of container, image, network, or volume.

Events are tagged on the wire with `"resource"` (`container` | `image` |
`network` | `volume`) so consumers can route on resource type without
matching the variant shape.

DaemonModeDto.description:
Coarse classification of the daemon's effective execution environment.

Mirror of [`zlayer_agent::capability::DaemonMode`]. The agent's enum lives
in a crate that doesn't depend on `utoipa`, so we duplicate the variants
here (kept in sync via the [`From`] impl below) and tag this one with
[`ToSchema`] for `OpenAPI` generation.

DeploymentDetails.description: Deployment details (enhanced with per-service health and endpoints)

oneOf.description: Deployment created but not yet started
Deployment is being rolled out
All services are running
Deployment failed with an error message
Deployment has been stopped

force.description: If true, the runtime is asked to forcibly detach.

confirm.description: Confirmation string -- must be `"CONFIRM_FORCE_LEADER"` to prevent accidents

preserved_nodes.description: Number of cluster nodes whose state was preserved

preserved_services.description: Number of services whose state was preserved

GitCredentialResponse.description: Git credential metadata (returned by list/create; no secret value).

GossipPeerSummary.description:
Snapshot of one gossip-pool peer, returned by
`GET /api/v1/cluster/gossip/peers`.

labels.description: Free-form labels advertised by the peer.

overlay_ip.description: Overlay IP assigned to this peer, if known.

GpuInfoSummary.description:
Summary of a GPU on a node, stored in Raft cluster state and reported
via cluster join requests.

Lifted from `zlayer-scheduler::raft` so that wire-types consumers
(zlayer-api DTOs, the CLI, the manager UI) can describe GPU inventory
without depending on the heavier scheduler crate. `zlayer-scheduler`
re-exports this type for source compatibility.

GpuUtilizationReport.description: Per-GPU utilization snapshot reported in node heartbeats

memory_used_mb.description: GPU memory currently used in MB

resource_id.description: A specific resource id, or omit for a wildcard (all resources of that kind).

subject_kind.description: Whether the subject is a user or a group.

members.description: List of user ids in the group.

HealthCheckRequest.description:
Container health check request.

Mirrors the on-disk `HealthCheck` enum (see `zlayer_spec::HealthCheck`) as a
discriminated union keyed on `type`. Translated to `zlayer_spec::HealthSpec`
by `HealthCheckRequest::to_health_spec`. Durations are humantime strings
(for example `"10s"`, `"500ms"`, `"1m"`).

## Variants
- `type: "tcp"` — requires `port` (1-65535).
- `type: "http"` — requires `url`; `expect_status` defaults to 200.
- `type: "command"` — requires `command` (array of argv tokens; joined with
  spaces and passed to `sh -c` by the health monitor, matching the existing
  compose-to-ZLayer conversion in `zlayer-docker`).

command.description:
Command argv (required when `type == "command"`). Joined with spaces
and passed to `sh -c`.

expect_status.description: HTTP status code expected from `url` (defaults to 200).

interval.description: Interval between checks, humantime format (e.g. `"30s"`). Defaults to 30s.

retries.description: Number of consecutive failures before marking unhealthy. Defaults to 3.

start_period.description:
Grace period before the first check runs, humantime format. Maps to
`HealthSpec::start_grace`.

timeout.description: Timeout per individual check, humantime format.

HeartbeatRequest.description: Heartbeat request from a worker node.

source.description: Source reference for `Tag` events (the image being aliased).

ImageInfoDto.description:
Serializable wrapper for `zlayer_agent::runtime::ImageInfo` so we can
attach `ToSchema` here (the underlying type in `zlayer-agent` can't
depend on `utoipa`).

InternalScaleRequest.description:
Wire-format scale request fanned out by the cluster's leader to each node
that gets assigned at least one replica of a service.

The leader's placement engine produces a `HashMap<NodeId, Vec<(role, index)>>`
of assignments; each entry becomes one `InternalScaleRequest` HTTP POST.

Backward-compatible: a peer may send `{service, replicas}` without
`assignments`. Receiving nodes treat that as a single implicit
`{role: "default", indices: 0..replicas}` group.

assignments.description:
Per-role-group container index lists. Empty in Phase 1; populated by
Phase 2 once `replica_groups` + cross-node identity ship.

replicas.description:
Total target replica count for this node, when caller didn't supply
explicit per-role assignments (legacy / Phase 1 shape). When
`assignments` is non-empty, this field is informational.

reroute_to_os.description:
When set, this agent refused the scale because it cannot run the
workload's OS (H-7 `RouteToPeer` policy). The value is the OCI-canonical
OS string the workload requires (`linux` / `windows` / `darwin`). The
scheduler catches this and re-dispatches to a cluster peer whose
`NodeState.os` matches.

allocated_ips.description: List of allocated IP addresses (only included if requested)

total_ips.description: Total available IPs in the range

status.description: Current status (pending, initializing, running, completed, failed, cancelled)

JoinTokenResponse.description: Join token response for cluster joining

allocated_ip.description: Allocated IP address for the joining node

leader_public_key.description: Leader's public key for secure communication

token.description: Join token for authenticating the new node

KillContainerRequest.description:
Request body for killing (sending a signal to) a container. Matches the
Docker-compat `POST /containers/{id}/kill` shape.

signal.description:
Signal name to send (e.g. `"SIGTERM"`, `"SIGINT"`). Accepts both the
`SIG`-prefixed and bare forms. When omitted, defaults to `SIGKILL`.

LifecycleSpec.description:
Lifecycle policy for service / job / cron containers.

Currently exposes a single `delete_on_exit` knob that, when `true`,
instructs higher layers to remove the container record (and its bundle)
once it has terminated. Other layers consume this field; this type is
purely descriptive.

delete_on_exit.description:
When true, terminated containers (and their bundles) are removed
automatically rather than retained for inspection. Defaults to
`false`, preserving the historical retain-on-exit behavior.

deployment_name.description: Name of the deployment to link.

enabled.description: Whether NAT traversal is enabled in the daemon's config

last_refresh.description: Unix epoch seconds of the last successful STUN refresh

relay_server_bind.description: Address of the locally-bound built-in relay server, if running

NetworkAttachmentInfo.description:
Per-network attachment entry on [`ContainerInfo::networks`].

Populated from the runtime's inspect response — mirrors the subset of
bollard's `EndpointSettings` that API clients need to correlate a container
with its `container_networks` entries.

aliases.description: DNS aliases the container answers to on this network.

ipv4.description: Assigned IPv4 on this network, if any.

network.description:
Network name as reported by the runtime. Matches the `name` field on
entries returned by `GET /api/v1/container-networks`.

NetworkAttachmentRequest.description:
A request to attach a freshly-created container to a user-defined bridge
or overlay network, mirroring the wire-shape used by `POST
/api/v1/container-networks/{id_or_name}/connect`.

Included on [`CreateContainerRequest::networks`] so callers can wire up
every attachment in a single call instead of issuing a separate connect
request per network after container create.

aliases.description: Optional DNS aliases for this container on the network.

ipv4_address.description:
Optional static IPv4 to pin this container to. Validated as
[`std::net::Ipv4Addr`] before the runtime is called.

network.description: Bridge-network id or name to attach to.

oneOf.description: Default networking (overlay / bridge as configured by the platform).
Share the host network namespace (Docker `--network host`).
Disable networking entirely (Docker `--network none`).

Attach to a Docker bridge network. When `name` is `None`, uses the
default `bridge` network.

bridge.description:
Attach to a Docker bridge network. When `name` is `None`, uses the
default `bridge` network.

oneOf.description:
Attach to another container's network namespace
(Docker `--network container:<id>`).

container.description:
Attach to another container's network namespace
(Docker `--network container:<id>`).

NetworkMode.description:
Network mode for a service container.

Mirrors Docker's `HostConfig.NetworkMode` semantics. Accepts both an
enum-tagged form (e.g. `network_mode: { bridge: { name: my-net } }`) and a
string form (e.g. `"host"`, `"bridge:my-net"`, `"container:abc123"`).

oneOf.description: Explicit allow-list of `node_id`s.

node_ids.description: The set of `node_id`s entitled to host this secret.

oneOf.description:
Match by node labels — every entry in the map must be present on
the node. Empty map matches every node.

NodeAffinity.description:
Constrains which nodes are allowed to host a given secret's
decryptable form. Used as the value of `ReplicatedSecret.node_affinity`.
`None` on a secret = unconstrained (any node may host); `Some(...)`
= only matching nodes receive a wrap of this row's DEK material,
and the API gate filters reads accordingly.

registered_at.description: When the node was registered (Unix timestamp)

NodeSelector.description: Node selection constraints for service placement

labels.description: Required labels that nodes must have (all must match)

prefer_labels.description: Preferred labels (soft constraint, nodes with these are preferred)

oneOf.description: Slack incoming webhook configuration.
Discord webhook configuration.
Generic HTTP webhook configuration.

headers.description: Extra headers to send with the request.

oneOf.description: SMTP email configuration.

OidcCallbackResponse.description:
Callback response body (returned as JSON unless the operator prefers a
redirect; initial integration keeps JSON so the Manager UI can show a
welcome screen post-exchange).

OidcProviderPublic.description:
Public-facing projection — what the Manager UI needs to render a login
button. Never includes the client secret.

OsKind.description:
Operating system a service needs to run on.

Mirrors the OS half of an OCI platform descriptor. Canonical wire strings
match Go's `GOOS` values (e.g. `"linux"`, `"windows"`, `"darwin"`).

OverlayMode.description:
How the daemon places a service's overlay attachment.

See module docs for the v0.51 implementation status of each variant.

is_leader.description: Whether this node is the cluster leader

last_check.description: Last health check timestamp (unix epoch seconds)

failure_count.description: Number of consecutive health check failures

last_check.description: Last health check timestamp (unix epoch seconds)

PermissionLevel.description:
Access level for a resource permission, ordered from least to most
privilege.

PortMapping.description:
A single host-to-container port publish rule (Docker's `-p`).

When `host_port` is `None` (or explicitly `Some(0)`), the container runtime
assigns an ephemeral host port. `host_ip` defaults to `"0.0.0.0"` to bind
on all interfaces.

host_ip.description: Host interface to bind on. Defaults to `"0.0.0.0"` (all interfaces).

host_port.description: Host port. `None` (or zero) means "assign an ephemeral port".

PortProtocol.description: Transport protocol for a published container port.

branch.description: Branch checked out in the working copy.

git_url.description: Git URL that was cloned / fetched.

path.description: Absolute path to the working copy on disk.

project_id.description: Project id the pull was performed for.

sha.description: HEAD commit SHA after the pull.

deleted.description: Image references or digests that were removed.

PullImageRequest.description: Request body for the pull-image handler. Blocking pull of an OCI image.

pull_policy.description:
Pull policy override. Accepts `"always"`, `"if_not_present"`, or
`"never"`. Defaults to `"always"` when omitted.

reference.description: OCI image reference to pull, e.g. `docker.io/library/nginx:latest`.

oneOf.description:
Inline Docker/OCI registry credentials used for this pull only. Not
persisted, never logged, never echoed back on a response. Takes
precedence over `registry_credential_id`.

registry_credential_id.description:
Id of a persisted registry credential (from
`POST /api/v1/credentials/registry`) to use for this pull. Ignored
when [`Self::registry_auth`] is also supplied (inline auth wins).
Requires the daemon to be configured with a credential store.

PullImageResponse.description:
Response body for the pull-image handler. Reports the pulled reference
and, when the backend exposes it via `list_images`, the resolved digest
and on-disk size.

digest.description: Content-addressed digest (`sha256:...`) if the runtime reports one.

size_bytes.description: On-disk size in bytes if the runtime reports one.

RegisteredServiceInfo.description: Information about a registered service in a tunnel

remote_port.description: Remote port exposed on the server

RegistryAuth.description:
Inline Docker/OCI registry credentials attached to a single pull request.

Prefer persistent credentials via `/api/v1/credentials/registry` for
long-lived services. Use this inline form for one-off pulls (e.g. CI
runners fetching a private image for a single job) where persisting a
credential is undesirable.

auth_type.description: Which authentication scheme to use against the registry.

password.description:
Password or bearer token. **Never** logged or returned on any
response — consumed once and dropped.

username.description:
Username for the registry (for basic auth) or a placeholder
identifier when `auth_type == Token`.

RegistryAuthTypeSchema.description: Authentication method for a registry credential (`OpenAPI` schema).

RegistryCredentialResponse.description: Registry credential metadata (returned by list/create; no password).

ReplicationInfo.description: Replication detail within the storage status response

last_sync.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last successful sync, or null

pending_changes.description: Number of WAL segments pending upload

RestartContainerRequest.description:
Request body for restarting a container. Matches the Docker-compat
`POST /containers/{id}/restart` shape.

timeout.description:
Graceful shutdown timeout in seconds before the runtime force-kills
the container. Defaults to 30 seconds when omitted.

RevealAllSecretsResponse.description:
Response for the batch reveal endpoint — returns every secret in an env as plaintext.
Admin-only for now (Phase 3 will gate this on per-env Read permission instead).

environment.description: The environment id the secrets were revealed from.

secrets.description: Name → plaintext value map. Includes every secret in the scope.

oneOf.description:
Optional per-secret node affinity update. `None` here means "leave
existing affinity unchanged"; to clear affinity explicitly, pass an
empty selector via a separate update endpoint (Phase 2).

value.description: The new secret value (will be encrypted at rest).

RotateSecretResponse.description: Response returned by the rotate endpoint.

previous_version.description:
Version prior to rotation. `None` if the secret did not exist (won't
happen today — rotate rejects missing secrets — but preserved for
forward compatibility).

RouteInfo.description: Information about a single registered route.

backends.description: Backend addresses currently assigned to this route.

host.description: Host pattern (e.g. `*.example.com`), or `null` for any host.

path_prefix.description: Path prefix matched by this route.

strip_prefix.description: Whether the matched prefix is stripped before forwarding.

ScaleAssignment.description:
One role-group entry within an [`InternalScaleRequest`]. Phase 2 ships
this; Phase 1 uses the legacy `replicas` field only.

indices.description:
Replica indices within this role that the receiving node should ensure
exist locally. Each becomes a container `{service}-{role}-{index}`.

SecretMetadataResponse.description:
Response containing secret metadata. Never includes the value unless
the caller is on the explicit `?reveal=true` admin path, in which case
`value` is populated.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp when the secret was created.

updated_at.description: Unix timestamp when the secret was last updated.

value.description: Plaintext value — populated only on `?reveal=true` admin reads.

version.description: Version number of the secret (incremented on each update).

ServiceHealthInfo.description: Per-service health info included in deployment details

ServiceOverlayStatus.description: Status of a service's overlay across the cluster.

bridges_by_node.description:
One entry per node where this service has at least one container
attached. Keys are node IDs as stringified.

mode.description: Mode the daemon resolved for this service (after `resolve_v0_51`).

current_password.description:
Required when the caller is setting their own password (non-admin
path). Admins changing someone else's password may omit this.

StepResult.description: Result of executing a single step in a workflow run.

StopContainerRequest.description:
Request body for stopping a container. Matches the Docker-compat
`POST /containers/{id}/stop` shape.

timeout.description:
Graceful shutdown timeout in seconds before the runtime force-kills
the container. Defaults to 30 seconds when omitted.

spec.description: The deployment specification (complex nested structure, see spec docs)

updated_at.description: When the deployment was last updated

StoredEnvironment.description:
A deployment/runtime environment (e.g. "dev", "staging", "prod").

Each environment is an isolated namespace for secrets and, later,
deployments. Optionally belongs to a `Project` (added in Phase 5) — when
`project_id` is `None`, the environment is global.

description.description: Free-form description shown in the UI.

name.description: Display name (e.g. "dev"). Unique within a given `project_id`.

project_id.description: Project id this environment belongs to. `None` = global.

updated_at.description: When the environment was last updated.

StoredNotifier.description:
A stored notifier — a named notification channel that fires alerts to
Slack, Discord, a generic webhook, or SMTP when triggered.

config.description: Channel-specific configuration (webhook URL, SMTP settings, etc.).

enabled.description: Whether this notifier is active. Disabled notifiers are skipped.

updated_at.description: When the notifier was last updated.

StoredPermission.description:
A stored permission grant binding a subject (user or group) to a resource
with a specific access level.

Canonical `resource_kind` strings (kept here for grep-ability):
- `"environment"` — a `StoredEnvironment` row.
- `"deployment"` — a `StoredDeployment` row.
- `"project"` — a `StoredProject` row.
- `"secret"` — a row in the secrets store (`{scope}:{name}` keyed).
- `"node"` — a cluster member identified by `NodeIdentity::node_id`.

id.description: UUID identifier of this permission grant.

resource_id.description:
A specific resource id, or `None` for a wildcard (all resources of
that kind).

subject_kind.description: Whether the subject is a user or a group.

StoredProject.description:
A project bundles a git source, build configuration, registry credential
reference, linked deployments, and a default environment.

auto_deploy.description:
Whether new commits on the tracked branch should automatically
trigger a build + deploy cycle.

oneOf.description: How the project is built.

default_environment_id.description: Reference to the default environment for this project.

deploy_spec_path.description:
Relative path (inside the cloned repo) to a `DeploymentSpec` YAML that
the workflow `DeployProject` action should apply.

When `None`, the workflow `DeployProject` action fails with a clear
"no deploy spec configured" error rather than silently succeeding —
callers are expected to set this explicitly via `project edit`.

description.description: Free-form description shown in the UI.

poll_interval_secs.description:
If set, the daemon polls the remote for new commits every N seconds.
`None` disables polling (the project is only updated via manual pull
or webhook).

updated_at.description: When the project was last updated.

StoredSync.description:
A stored sync resource (persistent record of a git-backed resource set).

A sync points at a directory within a project's checkout that contains
`ZLayer` resource YAMLs. On diff/apply the directory is scanned, compared
against current API state, and reconciled.

auto_apply.description: Whether the sync should automatically apply on pull.

delete_missing.description:
Whether the sync apply should delete resources that are present on the
API but missing from the manifest directory. Defaults to `false` —
the safer behaviour, which skips deletes and only creates/updates.

git_path.description: Path within the project's checkout to scan for resource YAMLs.

last_applied_sha.description: The commit SHA at which this sync was last applied.

project_id.description: Linked project id (the git checkout to scan).

updated_at.description: When the sync was last updated.

StoredTask.description:
A stored task — a named runnable script that can be executed on demand.

Tasks can be global (`project_id = None`) or project-scoped
(`project_id = Some(project_id)`).

project_id.description: Project id this task belongs to. `None` = global.

updated_at.description: When the task was last updated.

StoredUserGroup.description: A stored user group for role-based access control.

updated_at.description: When the group was last updated.

StoredVariable.description:
A stored variable — a plaintext key-value pair for template substitution
in deployment specs. Variables are NOT encrypted (unlike secrets). They
live in their own storage, separate from the encrypted secrets store.

Variables can be global (`scope = None`) or project-scoped
(`scope = Some(project_id)`).

name.description:
Variable name (e.g. `"APP_VERSION"`, `"LOG_LEVEL"`). Unique within a
given scope.

scope.description: Scope: project id or `None` for global.

updated_at.description: When the variable was last updated.

StoredWorkflow.description:
A stored workflow — a named sequence of steps forming a DAG that
composes tasks, project builds, deploys, and sync applies.

steps.description: Ordered list of steps to execute sequentially.

updated_at.description: When the workflow was last updated.

StreamBackendInfo.description: Information about a single L4 stream proxy backend.

StreamInfo.description: Information about a single L4 stream proxy.

SubjectKind.description: Whether a permission subject is a user or a group.

SyncApplyResponse.description:
Response for a real apply. Reports per-resource outcomes, the current
commit SHA the sync was applied at (when known), and a short human-readable
summary for CLI display.

NOTE: This is a breaking change from the previous dry-run-only
`{ diff, message }` shape. Clients inspecting the apply response directly
must be updated — callers that only consumed the HTTP status stay working.

applied_sha.description: Commit SHA the sync was applied against (when resolvable).

summary.description:
Aggregate summary suitable for CLI output, e.g.
`"3 created, 2 updated, 1 deleted, 0 skipped"`.

SyncDiffResponse.description: JSON-friendly wrapper around the sync diff output.

SyncResourceResponse.description: A single resource in the diff output.

SyncResourceResult.description: Result of reconciling a single resource during apply.

error.description:
Optional error message (`status == "error"`) or skip reason
(`action == "skip"`). Omitted on successful `"ok"` results.

resource.description: The source manifest path (or remote resource name for deletions).

TagImageRequest.description:
Request body for the tag-image handler. Matches Docker-compat
`docker tag` semantics: create a new reference (`target`) pointing at an
already-cached image (`source`).

TargetPlatform.description:
Platform a service targets. `None` on `ServiceSpec.platform` means
"any agent is acceptable" (preserves backward compatibility).

osVersion.description:
Optional OS version constraint — primarily for Windows multi-platform
images, where `platform.os.version` in the OCI index distinguishes build
families (e.g. `10.0.26100.*` for Server 2025 / Win11 24H2,
`10.0.20348.*` for Server 2022). When set on a Windows target the
registry platform resolver prefers manifest entries whose `os.version`
matches this value exactly or shares a `major.minor.build` prefix.
Unused on Linux/macOS platforms.

TaskRun.description: A recorded execution of a task.

exit_code.description: Process exit code (`None` if the task could not be started).

finished_at.description: When the run finished (`None` if it has not finished yet).

detect_files.description: Files that indicate this runtime should be used

success.description: Whether the test notification was sent successfully.

TriggerCronResponse.description: Response after triggering a cron job

created_at.description: When the tunnel was created (Unix timestamp)

last_connected.description: Last time the tunnel connected (Unix timestamp, if ever)

created_at.description: When the tunnel was created (Unix timestamp)

last_connected.description: Last time the tunnel connected (Unix timestamp, if ever)

UpdateEnvironmentRequest.description: Body for `PATCH /api/v1/environments/{id}`. All fields are optional.

description.description: New description. Pass `Some("")` to clear, omit to leave unchanged.

name.description: New display name. Will be re-checked for uniqueness.

oneOf.description: Updated configuration.

UpdateProjectRequest.description: Body for `PATCH /api/v1/projects/{id}`. All fields are optional.

auto_deploy.description: Enable or disable automatic deploy on new commits.

oneOf.description: New build kind.

deploy_spec_path.description:
New path (inside the cloned repo) to the `DeploymentSpec` YAML that
workflow `DeployProject` actions should apply. Pass `""` to clear.

poll_interval_secs.description: Set polling interval in seconds. `null` disables polling.

UpdateUserRequest.description:
Body for `PATCH /api/v1/users/{id}`. All fields are optional so a caller
can update a single attribute without touching the others.

UpdateVariableRequest.description: Body for `PATCH /api/v1/variables/{id}`. All fields are optional.

name.description: New variable name. Will be re-checked for uniqueness.

UpgradeError.description: Per-node error captured during a rolling upgrade.

UpgradeJobState.description:
State of a single daemon-binary upgrade attempt. Stored in
[`InternalState::upgrade_jobs`] and returned by `GET
/api/v1/internal/upgrade/{id}`.

error.description: Human-readable failure reason (set only when `status == Failed`).

finished_at.description: When the job entered its terminal state (`Restarting` or `Failed`).

version.description:
Target version. `None` (or `"latest"`) defers to `self-update`'s
"latest GitHub release" resolver.

UpgradeStatus.description:
Lifecycle of a daemon-binary upgrade job, tracked in-memory on the node
being upgraded.

UserRole.description:
User role. Admins can do everything; regular users are constrained by
per-resource permissions (added in a later phase).

UserView.description: Response shape used by login + bootstrap + me.

VolumeInfo.description:
Full volume response shape used by the list, inspect, and create
endpoints.

created_at.description:
RFC 3339 creation timestamp. For volumes without a sidecar this is
the directory's mtime (best-effort).

in_use_by.description:
Container IDs currently mounting this volume. Empty when no
`VolumeUsageSource` is wired.

labels.description: Labels from the sidecar. Empty when no sidecar is present.

size_bytes.description:
Approximate size in bytes (sum of regular files in the volume
directory). `None` when the directory could not be walked, or for
freshly created empty volumes where `0` would be equally
informative.

VolumeMount.description:
Volume mount specification.

The `type` field (a Docker-compatible discriminator) selects how `source`
is interpreted:
- `"bind"` (default): `source` is an absolute host path.
- `"volume"`: `source` is a named-volume identifier.
- `"tmpfs"`: no `source`; a memory-backed mount is provisioned.

source.description: Host path (bind), volume name (volume), or unused (tmpfs).

oneOf.description: Mount kind. Omit (or `"bind"`) for legacy host-path binds.

VolumeMountType.description:
Volume mount kind discriminator.

Selects which [`zlayer_spec::StorageSpec`] variant [`VolumeMount`] is
translated into by [`build_service_spec`]. When omitted on the wire,
defaults to [`VolumeMountType::Bind`] (legacy behavior).

VolumeSummary.description:
Legacy response shape kept for backwards compatibility with older SDK
consumers that deserialize strictly. New consumers should use
[`VolumeInfo`]. `list_volumes` now returns [`VolumeInfo`] which is a
strict superset of the fields in `VolumeSummary`.

secret.description: The HMAC secret to paste into the git host.

url.description: Full URL to configure in the git host's webhook settings.

WebhookResponse.description: Response for the public webhook receiver.

sha.description: HEAD commit SHA after the pull.

WorkerSummary.description: Summary of a worker-tier worker node, returned by `GET /api/v1/cluster/workers`.

last_seen_unix_secs.description: Last time the leader observed the worker.

oneOf.description: Execute a task by id.
Build a project by id.
Deploy a project by id.
Apply a sync resource by id.

WorkflowAction.description: The action a workflow step performs.

WorkflowRun.description: A recorded execution of a workflow.

finished_at.description: When the run finished (`None` if still running).

WorkflowRunStatus.description: Overall status of a workflow run.

WorkflowStep.description: A single step in a workflow.

on_failure.description: Name of another step (or task id) to run on failure.
